L1.area: Netfilter batch message construction and network communication layer

L2.description: A heap-based buffer overflow exists where a crafted size parameter causes an arithmetic overflow during buffer size calculation, resulting in an undersized memory allocation and subsequent out-of-bounds writes during initialization of a message batching structure.

L3.area: Netfilter batch buffer allocation and initialization logic
L3.description: A heap-based buffer overflow exists where a user-provided page size is added to the maximum netlink message size without overflow checking. When this u32 addition wraps around, the underlying C library receives an insufficient allocation size, leading to heap out-of-bounds writes during batch buffer initialization.

ground_truth.reason: The Batch::with_page_size constructor passes a user-controlled page size to the underlying C allocation without checking for integer overflow, allowing a crafted value to wrap the u32 addition and cause an undersized heap allocation with subsequent out-of-bounds writes.
